description_long: This object is a four-panel folding room divider, often referred to as a shoji screen, featuring a wooden frame with a grid pattern and translucent white panels. The frame is a warm brown color, and each panel is connected by hinges, allowing the divider to be folded or expanded as needed. The grid pattern is created by thin wooden slats that divide each panel into rectangular sections, giving it a traditional Japanese aesthetic. The translucent panels are likely made of rice paper or a similar lightweight material, allowing light to pass through while providing privacy. The overall construction appears lightweight yet sturdy, suitable for indoor use in homes, offices, or urban spaces for temporary partitioning. The design is minimalist and functional, blending well with various interior styles.
description: A four-panel folding shoji screen with a brown wooden frame and translucent white grid-patterned panels. It serves as a stylish and functional room divider.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full expanse of the four connected panels with the grid pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the divider and the zigzag folding structure of the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the grid pattern and panel connections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the slim, folded profile and the panel arrangement.
